questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: Which of the following is a mandatory eligibility requirement for a sportsperson to be considered for the Major Dhyan Chand Khel Ratna Award?

options.text: The sportsperson must have scored at least 33 goals in international matches.
The sportsperson must have a clean anti-doping record.
The sportsperson must be a citizen of more than one country.
The sportsperson must have received the Arjuna Award previously.

questions.explanation: A clean anti-doping record is a specific eligibility criterion mentioned for the Khel Ratna. Option A refers to a historical fact about Dhyan Chand himself, not a requirement for current athletes. Option C is incorrect as it is an Indian national honor. Option D is not a mandatory prerequisite mentioned in the text.
questions.takeaway: Ethical compliance, specifically anti-doping, is a core requirement for India's highest sporting honors.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Eligibility: Exceptional international performance over four years. Clean anti-doping record.
